Efficacy and safety of berberine in preventing recurrence of colorectal adenomas.

Nut consumption was significantly associated with a reduced risk of cancer incidence.
Preoperative C-reactive protein and its related markers, GPS and mGPS, were significantly associated with the survival of colorectal cancer surgery patients.
Prolonged television viewing, occupational sitting time, and total sitting time are associated with increased risks of colorectal cancer.
Review: there is a consistently strong inverse correlations with solar UVB for 15 types of cancers.
Specific combinations of traditional medicines appeared to produce higher contributions to tumor response rate than the TMs individually.

These analysis indicates the potential benefits of flavan-3-ols in cancer prevention.

A combination of apigenin and EGCG prevent colorectal cancer recurrence.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Essentially zero; 5-year survival stage I: ~90% (resectable); stage IV: ~15% overall, ~20-30% with modern therapy and liver metastasis resection
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Stage I-III with surgery + adjuvant chemotherapy: 60-80% 5-year survival; MSI-H/dMMR CRC: pembrolizumab first-line achieves ~55% 5-year PFS vs ~16% with chemotherapy (KEYNOTE-177); complete tumour regression with neoadjuvant therapy in rectal cancer (watch-and-wait): 15-30% complete pathological response allowing organ preservation
remission.gap_size: Large screening gap: only 60-68% of US adults up-to-date with CRC screening; large treatment gap for MSI-H biomarker testing
remission.barrier_detail: Colonoscopy screening is dramatically underused despite proven 60-70% mortality reduction; stool-based tests (FIT, Cologuard) improving uptake but sensitivity/specificity inferior; biomarker testing for MSI/MMR status still not universal; palliative colostomy vs sphincter-preserving approaches vary widely by provider
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — pembrolizumab first-line for MSI-H/dMMR mCRC standard of care (KEYNOTE-177); LONSURF (trifluridine/tipiracil) and fruquintinib for refractory disease; bevacizumab + FOLFOX/FOLFIRI backbone since 2004 essentially unchanged; EGFR inhibitors (cetuximab) in RAS wild-type since 2012
remission.outdated_treatments: 5-FU alone without leucovorin or oxaliplatin in eligible patients; clinical follow-up without CEA monitoring and CT surveillance post-resection; liver metastasis deemed unresectable without multidisciplinary surgical review (resectability borderline — many patients excluded who could benefit from downsizing with chemotherapy)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise (reduces CRC recurrence risk 25-35% in observational data; CHALLENGE trial underway); aspirin as adjuvant for PIK3CA-mutant CRC (BANDOC trial); dietary: high-fibre, low-red meat dietary patterns post-diagnosis; probiotic microbiome optimisation during chemotherapy
remission.frontier_signal: Watch-and-wait after complete response in rectal cancer (organ preservation); ctDNA-guided adjuvant therapy (CIRCULATE-Japan, DYNAMIC-III); tumour-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) therapy for MSS CRC; mRNA vaccines (mRNA-5671 targeting KRAS mutations, Phase 1)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
